User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the ShopManager application with specific roles and access permissions. Authentication details (like passwords) are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: User's email address, used for identification and login.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The assigned role of the user, determining their access level. Expected values are 'Admin' or 'Seller'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for products, allowing for organized product catalogs (e.g., keyboard, mouse, screen).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the product category.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an item available for sale, including its inventory details, pricing, and associated metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

purchasePrice.description: The price at which the product was acquired by the seller.

sellingPrice.description: The standard price at which the product is sold to customers.

stockQuantity.description: The current number of units of this product available in inventory.

imageUrl.description: URL of the primary image representing the product.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer who places orders through the public-facing store or whose details are recorded during POS sales.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the customer.

lastName.description: The last name of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

phoneNumber.description: The primary phone number of the customer.

address.description: The physical address of the customer, potentially for shipping.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was last updated.

PaymentMode.title: PaymentMode
PaymentMode.description: Defines the different methods customers can use to pay for their purchases (e.g., Cash, Pi Network, Mobile Money).

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentMode entity.

name.description: The display name of the payment mode (e.g., 'Pi Network', 'Mobile Money - Orange', 'Cash').

type.description: The general classification of the payment mode (e.g., 'Crypto', 'Mobile Money', 'Cash').

Sale.title: Sale
Sale.description: Represents a single completed transaction, encompassing details from both POS sales and customer orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sale transaction.

saleDate.description: The date and time when the sale or order was finalized.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary value of all items in this sale.

paymentModeId.description: Reference to the PaymentMode used for this transaction. (Relationship: PaymentMode 1:N Sale)

sellerId.description: Reference to the User (Seller) who processed this sale via POS. Can be null for direct customer orders. (Relationship: User 1:N Sale)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who placed this order. Can be null for walk-in POS sales. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Sale)

status.description: The current status of the sale or order. Expected values are 'pending', 'completed', or 'cancelled'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the sale record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the sale record was last updated.

SaleItem.title: SaleItem
SaleItem.description: Represents a specific product and its quantity within a larger Sale transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SaleItem entity.

saleId.description: Reference to the Sale transaction this item belongs to. (Relationship: Sale 1:N SaleItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was sold. (Relationship: Product 1:N SaleItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of the product sold in this particular item.

priceAtSale.description: The price of a single unit of the product recorded at the time of the sale, to ensure historical accuracy.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is typically restricted to the user themselves (for their own document) and administrators. Note: The 'role' field here is for informational/UI purposes; actual authorization roles are determined by existence in `/admins` or `/sellers` collections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for administrative role assignment. The existence of a document here (with `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid`) grants administrative privileges. This design ensures Authorization Independence (DBAC - Existence over Content).

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user with 'Admin' role, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for seller role assignment. The existence of a document here (with `sellerId` matching `request.auth.uid`) grants seller privileges. This design ensures Authorization Independence (DBAC - Existence over Content).

params.name: sellerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user with 'Seller' role, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores product categories. Publicly readable for customer browsing. Write access restricted to Admin and Seller roles.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a product category.

definition.description: Stores product details and inventory. Publicly readable for customer browsing. Write access restricted to Admin and Seller roles.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a product.

definition.description: Stores customer information. Read/Write access restricted to Admin and Seller roles.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for a customer.

definition.description: Stores available payment methods. Readable by Admin and Seller roles. Write access restricted to Admin roles.

params.name: paymentModeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a payment mode.

definition.description: Stores details of individual sales transactions or customer orders. Includes `sellerId` and `customerId` fields for authorization. Readable by Admin, the associated Seller, or the associated Customer. Write access for Admin and associated Seller.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a sales transaction.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a sale transaction. Includes denormalized `sellerId` and `customerId` from the parent `Sale` for Authorization Independence, allowing direct security rule checks without a `get()` call to the parent. Readable by Admin, the associated Seller (via `sellerId`), or the associated Customer (via `customerId`). Write access for Admin and associated Seller.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent sales transaction.

params.name: saleItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific item within the sales transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and DBAC. Authentication and authorization are primarily driven by `request.auth.uid` and database-backed roles, eliminating the need for custom claims.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
-   **Global Roles:** Instead of relying on a `role` field within the main `/users/{userId}` document (which would require a `get()` call to check), dedicated collections `/admins/{adminId}` and `/sellers/{sellerId}` are used. The *existence* of a document with `request.auth.uid` in these collections grants the respective role. This ensures atomic and highly performant authorization checks without `get()` calls.
-   **Nested Data (`SaleItem`):** The `SaleItem` entity is a subcollection of `Sale` (`/sales/{saleId}/saleItems/{saleItemId}`). To avoid a `get()` call to the parent `Sale` document to determine who can access a `SaleItem` (e.g., the `sellerId` or `customerId` of the `Sale`), the critical authorization fields (`sellerId` and `customerId`) from the parent `Sale` are **denormalized** into each `SaleItem` document. This allows security rules on `saleItems` to directly check `request.resource.data.sellerId` or `request.resource.data.customerId`, maintaining authorization independence.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
-   **Publicly Accessible Data:** Collections like `/categories` and `/products` are structured for broad public read access, allowing unauthenticated users (customers) to browse. `list` queries on these collections will not be filtered by security rules but are inherently secure as they grant full public read.
-   **Role-Based Access:** Collections like `/users`, `/customers`, and `/paymentModes` are restricted to Admin/Seller roles. Any `list` query on these collections by unauthorized users will be rejected at the collection level, not filtered. Access to individual documents within these collections (e.g., `customers/{customerId}`) is secured based on the caller's role.
-   **User/Customer Specific Data:** The `/sales` collection includes `sellerId` and `customerId` fields directly in the document. This enables efficient and secure QAPs for `list` operations, allowing sellers to query `sales.where('sellerId', '==', request.auth.uid)` and customers to query `sales.where('customerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The security rules would ensure that `request.query.where` clauses match the user's `uid` for `sellerId` or `customerId` if not an admin. Similarly, for `saleItems`, the denormalized `sellerId` and `customerId` allow for secure queries based on these fields (e.g., for a specific sale `saleItems.where('sellerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`).

**Structural Segregation:**
-   Each collection generally serves a single security posture. For example, `products` are publicly readable for browsing, but write operations are restricted to `Admin` and `Seller` roles. There's no mixing of public posts and private drafts within the same collection. All `User` related data (profiles, roles) are logically separated into `users`, `admins`, `sellers` collections, making role-based access clear and easy to manage.

This design prioritizes robust security rules that are simple, efficient, and easy to debug by avoiding complex `get()` calls and ensuring that all necessary authorization context is available directly within the document being accessed or through simple existence checks on role collections.
